title: Design Rest Endpoint
prompt: Design a custom REST endpoint for a plugin that returns diagnostic status publicly and updates diagnostic settings privately. Include namespace, methods, schema, permission callbacks, response shape, and error behavior.
hints: Split public reads from private writes. Do not make one callback guess the intent.

model_answer.summary: Expose diagnostics as a public read contract and settings updates as a private write contract with different routes, schemas, and permissions.
model_answer.work: Register a namespace such as archive-faculty/v1. Add GET /status for safe public diagnostic data with a permission_callback that allows only bounded read-only output, and return fields such as plugin_version, schema_version, cache_status, and last_job_status. Add POST /settings for private updates with args schema, a permission_callback that checks current_user_can('manage_options') or a narrower capability, validation before storage, and WP_Error for unauthorized or invalid input. Keep response shapes structured and documented; do not echo directly or mix public reads and private writes in one ambiguous callback.
checks: Verify GET /status works publicly and does not expose secrets.
Verify POST /settings denies anonymous and low-capability users.
Test schema validation failures and WP_Error response shape.
Confirm public and private routes have separate permission_callback behavior.
Add REST integration tests for successful and denied requests.
model_answer.why_this_is_good: It prevents a common REST mistake: a single route that silently becomes a write path. The public/private boundary is visible in the route design and test plan.
common_misses: One route that reads or writes depending on payload.
Public diagnostics that expose tokens, raw requests, or secrets.
Ad hoc echoes instead of structured REST responses.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by splitting the read and write surfaces, naming the permission_callback for each, and listing the response and error contract.

summary.description: One or two sentences explaining the answer.

work.description: The main implementation, review, or design answer.

checks.description: Verification steps, review checks, or acceptance criteria.
